_comment: Caveau guard configuration. Copy to one of the search locations (see README) as 'caveau-guard.json' and edit. All paths may be absolute or use ~ for the home directory. The guard is FAIL-CLOSED: if this file is malformed it errors loudly rather than silently letting data through.
_protected_folders_doc: Any Read/Grep/Glob/Edit/Write/Bash/Notebook tool call that touches a file INSIDE one of these folders (recursively) is DENIED. This is where raw client PII lives — the 'coffre'. Claude must run the data through Caveau first, then work on the anonymised copy.
_allow_extensions_doc: Optional escape hatch: file extensions inside a protected folder that are SAFE to read directly (e.g. '.anon.txt' for already-cloaked output, or '.md' for your own notes). Empty = nothing is exempted, everything in the folder is blocked. Use sparingly.
_allow_paths_doc: Optional list of specific paths (files or sub-folders) inside a protected folder that are explicitly allowed despite the parent being protected — e.g. a 'clean/' sub-folder holding anonymised output.
_block_bash_doc: When true, Bash commands that mention a protected path anywhere in the command string are also denied (stops `cat ~/Dossiers-clients/x.pdf` from bypassing the Read guard). Set false only if it produces too many false positives in a given deployment.
message_fr:
🔒 Caveau — accès bloqué.
Ce fichier est dans un dossier client protégé : les données identifiantes ne doivent jamais entrer dans le contexte de l'IA en clair.
→ Lance d'abord l'anonymisation Caveau sur ce dossier (skill /caveau-guard:caveau-anonymize), puis travaille sur la copie anonymisée. Le coffre (correspondance jeton ↔ vraie valeur) reste en local.
_tripwire_enabled_doc: The tripwire is a SECOND guard (UserPromptSubmit hook) for the chat box: it watches the prompt text for raw client PII (IBAN, email, n° sécu, téléphone) pasted directly, or phrasing that implies an uploaded/attached document. Unlike the folder guard, it can't anonymise an upload's content (the platform injects it into context before any hook runs) — so it NUDGES Claude to redirect the user to the protected folder. Set false to disable.
_tripwire_block_doc: When false (default), the tripwire only adds a soft instruction to Claude's context ('don't analyse raw pasted PII; tell the user to use the folder') — the user's prompt still goes through. When true, it HARD-BLOCKS the prompt (the message is erased and the user is told to use the folder). Hard-block is stricter but more disruptive and prone to false positives; enable only for strict-compliance setups.
